growth_records.snippet: Comparison of wild-type Enterobacter aerogenes IAM1183 and mutants Ea-1 to -4 in 20-h shake-flask fermentations using 40 g/L of glucose as the carbon source (n=3).
growth_records.notes: End-point concentrations (mM) at 20 h shake-flask, not specific rates; biomass reported only as OD600 in figures so mmol/gDW/h not derivable. Consumed carbon and products from Table 2/3/4. Medium ZnCl2 34.2 g/L stated verbatim (probable mg/L typo); MnCl2 amount not resolvable in source text.

growth_records.medium: Defined 2,3-BD production medium (Jung et al., 2012), glucose (~60 g/L inferred from % consumed vs residual)

up.units: % of supplied glucose consumed at 44 h

growth_records.cite: Wu Yan et al. Metabolic Engineering of Enterobacter aerogenes for Improved 2,3-Butanediol Production by Manipulating NADH Levels and Overexpressing the Small RNA RyhB. Frontiers in Microbiology (2021). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2021.754306. [PMC8531500]
growth_records.snippet: Analysis of consumed substrate and anaerobic metabolites after 44 h of cultivation of wild-type E. aerogenes IAM1183 and Ea-2 in 5-L fermenter.
growth_records.notes: Table 5 5-L fermenter, 44 h end-point. Glucose consumption given as % (IAM1183 86.74%, Ea-2 94.11%); residual sugar and products in mM. Initial glucose ~60 g/L inferred (residual/[1-fraction]); not asserted. Biomass only as OD600 in Figure 6, so specific rates not derivable.

growth_records.snippet: Analysis of consumed substrate and anaerobic metabolites after 44 h of cultivation of wild-type E. aerogenes IAM1183 and Ea-2 in 5-L fermenter.
growth_records.notes: Table 5 5-L fermenter, 44 h end-point. Glucose consumption given as % (IAM1183 86.74%, Ea-2 94.11%); residual sugar and products in mM. Initial glucose ~60 g/L inferred (residual/[1-fraction]); not asserted. Biomass only as OD600 in Figure 6, so specific rates not derivable.
